result.markdown:
--- title: "7a. Modelling, Visualising and Analysing Network Data with R" author: "carina peh" date: "May 30, 2026" date-modified: "last-modified" execute: eval: true echo: true warning: false freeze: true --- ## Overview Network data consists of two key components — **nodes** (entities) and **edges** (relationships between entities). In this exercise, email correspondence data from GAStech, an oil exploration company, is used to explore and visualise communication patterns between 55 employees over two weeks. ## Reflections Network visualisation uses nodes(data points) and edges (links) to visually map connected data, it reveals patterns that tabular data cannot such as which employees are central connectors, which departments communicate most, and whether communication clusters form around certain groups. The key takeaway is that `tidygraph` makes network data manipulation feel familiar using `dplyr` verbs, while `ggraph` extends `ggplot2` grammar to network graphs. The interactive `visNetwork` output added practical value by allowing exploration of individual nodes and edges dynamically. ## Getting Started ### Loading packages ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} pacman::p_load(igraph, tidygraph, ggraph, visNetwork, lubridate, clock, tidyverse, graphlayouts, concaveman, ggforce) ``` ::: ### Importing data ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} GAStech_nodes <- read_csv("GAStech_email_node.csv") GAStech_edges <- read_csv("GAStech_email_edge-v2.csv") ``` ::: ### Reviewing the data ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} glimpse(GAStech_edges) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Rows: 9,063 Columns: 8 $ source 43, 43, 44, 44, 44, 44, 44, 44, 44, 44, 44, 44, 26, 26, 26… $ target 41, 40, 51, 52, 53, 45, 44, 46, 48, 49, 47, 54, 27, 28, 29… $ SentDate "6/1/2014", "6/1/2014", "6/1/2014", "6/1/2014", "6/1/2014"… $ SentTime 08:39:00, 08:39:00, 08:58:00, 08:58:00, 08:58:00, 08:58:0… $ Subject "GT-SeismicProcessorPro Bug Report", "GT-SeismicProcessorP… $ MainSubject "Work related", "Work related", "Work related", "Work rela… $ sourceLabel "Sven.Flecha", "Sven.Flecha", "Kanon.Herrero", "Kanon.Herr… $ targetLabel "Isak.Baza", "Lucas.Alcazar", "Felix.Resumir", "Hideki.Coc… ``` ::: ::: ## Data Wrangling ### Wrangling time `SentDate` is imported as a character data type and needs to be converted to a proper date format. `dmy()` converts the date string and `wday()` extracts the day of the week — both from the **lubridate** package. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} GAStech_edges <- GAStech_edges %>% mutate(SendDate = dmy(SentDate)) %>% mutate(Weekday = wday(SentDate, label = TRUE, abbr = FALSE)) ``` ::: ### Wrangling attributes We filter to keep only work-related emails and aggregate by source, target and weekday to count the number of emails sent. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} GAStech_edges_aggregated <- GAStech_edges %>% filter(MainSubject == "Work related") %>% group_by(source, target, Weekday) %>% summarise(Weight = n()) %>% filter(source != target) %>% filter(Weight > 1) %>% ungroup() ``` ::: ## Creating Network Objects with tidygraph ### Building the tidygraph object `tbl_graph()` creates a network object from the nodes and edges dataframes. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} GAStech_graph <- tbl_graph(nodes = GAStech_nodes, edges = GAStech_edges_aggregated, directed = TRUE) GAStech_graph ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tbl_graph: 54 nodes and 1372 edges # # A directed multigraph with 1 component # # Node Data: 54 × 4 (active) id label Department Title 1 1 Mat.Bramar Administration Assistant to CEO 2 2 Anda.Ribera Administration Assistant to CFO 3 3 Rachel.Pantanal Administration Assistant to CIO 4 4 Linda.Lagos Administration Assistant to COO 5 5 Ruscella.Mies.Haber Administration Assistant to Engineering Group Mana… 6 6 Carla.Forluniau Administration Assistant to IT Group Manager 7 7 Cornelia.Lais Administration Assistant to Security Group Manager 8 44 Kanon.Herrero Security Badging Office 9 45 Varja.Lagos Security Badging Office 10 46 Stenig.Fusil Security Building Control # ℹ 44 more rows # # Edge Data: 1,372 × 4 from to Weekday Weight 1 1 2 Sunday 5 2 1 2 Monday 2 3 1 2 Tuesday 3 # ℹ 1,369 more rows ``` ::: ::: ### Changing the active object `tidygraph` allows switching between nodes and edges using `activate()`. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} GAStech_graph %>% activate(edges) %>% arrange(desc(Weight)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tbl_graph: 54 nodes and 1372 edges # # A directed multigraph with 1 component # # Edge Data: 1,372 × 4 (active) from to Weekday Weight 1 40 41 Saturday 13 2 41 43 Monday 11 3 35 31 Tuesday 10 4 40 41 Monday 10 5 40 43 Monday 10 6 36 32 Sunday 9 7 40 43 Saturday 9 8 41 40 Monday 9 9 19 15 Wednesday 8 10 35 38 Tuesday 8 # ℹ 1,362 more rows # # Node Data: 54 × 4 id label Department Title 1 1 Mat.Bramar Administration Assistant to CEO 2 2 Anda.Ribera Administration Assistant to CFO 3 3 Rachel.Pantanal Administration Assistant to CIO # ℹ 51 more rows ``` ::: ::: ## Plotting Static Network Graphs with ggraph ### Basic network graph ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex07a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggraph(GAStech_graph) + geom_edge_link() + geom_node_point() ``` ::: ::: ### With theme and colour ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex07a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} g <- ggraph(GAStech_graph, layout = "fr") + geom_edge_link(aes(colour = Weekday)) + geom_node_point(aes(colour = Department), size = 3) + theme_graph() g ``` ::: ::: ### Modifying edges by weight ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex07a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-12-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} g <- ggraph(GAStech_graph, layout = "nicely") + geom_edge_link(aes(width = Weight), alpha = 0.2) + scale_edge_width(range = c(0.1, 5)) + geom_node_point(aes(colour = Department), size = 3) + theme_graph() g ``` ::: ::: ## Creating Facet Graphs ### Facet by edges ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex07a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-14-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} set_graph_style() g <- ggraph(GAStech_graph, layout = "nicely") + geom_edge_link(aes(width = Weight), alpha = 0.2) + scale_edge_width(range = c(0.1, 5)) + geom_node_point(aes(colour = Department), size = 2) g + facet_edges(~Weekday) ``` ::: ::: ### Facet by nodes ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex07a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-16-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} set_graph_style() g <- ggraph(GAStech_graph, layout = "nicely") + geom_edge_link(aes(width = Weight), alpha = 0.2) + scale_edge_width(range = c(0.1, 5)) + geom_node_point(aes(colour = Department), size = 2) g + facet_nodes(~Department) + th_foreground(foreground = "grey80", border = TRUE) ``` ::: ::: ## Network Metrics Analysis ### Computing centrality indices `centrality_betweenness()` measures how often a node lies on the shortest path between other nodes — a key measure of influence. ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex07a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-18-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} g <- GAStech_graph %>% mutate(betweenness_centrality = centrality_betweenness()) %>% ggraph(layout = "fr") + geom_edge_link(aes(width = Weight), alpha = 0.2) + scale_edge_width(range = c(0.1, 5)) + geom_node_point(aes(colour = Department, size = betweenness_centrality)) + theme_graph() g ``` ::: ::: ### Visualising community `group_edge_betweenness()` detects communities — groups of nodes that are more connected to each other than to the rest of the network. ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex07a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-20-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} g <- GAStech_graph %>% mutate(community = as.factor( group_edge_betweenness(weights = Weight, directed = TRUE))) %>% ggraph(layout = "fr") + geom_edge_link(aes(width = Weight), alpha = 0.2) + scale_edge_width(range = c(0.1, 5)) + geom_node_point(aes(colour = community), size = 3) + theme_graph() g ``` ::: ::: ## Building Interactive Network Graph with visNetwork ### Data preparation `visNetwork` requires nodes to have an `id` column and edges to have `from` and `to` columns. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} GAStech_edges_aggregated <- GAStech_edges_aggregated %>% rename(from = source, to = target) GAStech_nodes <- GAStech_nodes %>% rename(id = id, label = label) ``` ::: ### Basic interactive graph ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} visNetwork(GAStech_nodes, GAStech_edges_aggregated) %>% visIgraphLayout(layout = "layout_with_fr") ``` ::: ::: ### With visual attributes and interactivity ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} GAStech_nodes <- GAStech_nodes %>% rename(group = Department) visNetwork(GAStech_nodes, GAStech_edges_aggregated) %>% visIgraphLayout(layout = "layout_with_fr") %>% visLegend() %>% visLayout(randomSeed = 123) ``` ::: :::
